children.heading: Purpose
children.label: a Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this section is to encourage qualified individuals to enter and continue employment as prosecutors and public defenders.

children.label: A
children.text: is continually licensed to practice law; and

children.label: B
children.text: prosecutes criminal or juvenile delinquency cases at the State or unit of local government level (including supervision, education, or training of other persons prosecuting such cases).

children.label: A
children.text: is continually licensed to practice law; and

children.label: i
children.text: a full-time employee of a State or unit of local government who provides legal representation to indigent persons in criminal or juvenile delinquency cases (including supervision, education, or training of other persons providing such representation);

children.label: ii
children.text: a full-time employee of a nonprofit organization operating under a contract with a State or unit of local government, who devotes substantially all of the employee’s full-time employment to providing legal representation to indigent persons in criminal or juvenile delinquency cases (including supervision, education, or training of other persons providing such representation); or

children.label: iii
children.text: employed as a full-time Federal defender attorney in a defender organization established pursuant to subsection (g) of that provides legal representation to indigent persons in criminal or juvenile delinquency cases.section 3006A of title 18

children.label: i
children.text: a loan made, insured, or guaranteed under part B of title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.);20 U.S.C. 1071

children.label: ii
children.text: a loan made under part D or E of title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq. and 1087aa et seq.); and20 U.S.C. 1087a

children.label: iii
children.text: a loan made under section 428C or 455(g) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( and 1087e(g)).20 U.S.C. 1078–3

children.heading: Exclusion of parent PLUS loans
children.label: B Exclusion of parent PLUS loans
i
children.text: A loan made to the parents of a dependent student under section 428B of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ().20 U.S.C. 1078–2

children.label: ii
children.text: A Federal Direct PLUS Loan made to the parents of a dependent student.

children.label: iii
children.text: A loan made under section 428C or 455(g) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( and 1087e(g)) to the extent that such loan was used to repay a loan described in clause (i) or (ii).20 U.S.C. 1078–3
is employed as a prosecutor or public defender; and
is not in default on a loan for which the borrower seeks forgiveness.

children.label: A
children.text: the borrower will remain employed as a prosecutor or public defender for a required period of service of not less than three years, unless involuntarily separated from that employment;

children.label: B
children.text: if the borrower is involuntarily separated from employment on account of misconduct, or voluntarily separates from employment, before the end of the period specified in the agreement, the borrower will repay the Attorney General the amount of any benefits received by such employee under this section;

children.label: C
children.text: if the borrower is required to repay an amount to the Attorney General under subparagraph (B) and fails to repay such amount, a sum equal to that amount shall be recoverable by the Federal Government from the employee (or such employee’s estate, if applicable) by such methods as are provided by law for the recovery of amounts owed to the Federal Government;

children.label: D
children.text: the Attorney General may waive, in whole or in part, a right of recovery under this subsection if it is shown that recovery would be against equity and good conscience or against the public interest; and

children.label: E
children.text: the Attorney General shall make student loan payments under this section for the period of the agreement, subject to the availability of appropriations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any amount repaid by, or recovered from, an individual or the estate of an individual under this subsection shall be credited to the appropriation account from which the amount involved was originally paid.

children.heading: Merger
children.label: B Merger
children.text: Any amount credited under subparagraph (A) shall be merged with other sums in such account and shall be available for the same purposes and period, and subject to the same limitations, if any, as the sums with which the amount was merged.

children.label: i
children.text: $10,000 for any borrower in any calendar year; or

children.label: ii
children.text: an aggregate total of $60,000 in the case of any borrower.

children.heading: Beginning of payments
children.label: B Beginning of payments
children.text: Nothing in this section shall authorize the Attorney General to pay any amount to reimburse a borrower for any repayments made by such borrower prior to the date on which the Attorney General entered into an agreement with the borrower under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: On completion of the required period of service under an agreement under subsection (d), the borrower and the Attorney General may, subject to paragraph (2), enter into an additional agreement in accordance with subsection (d).

children.heading: Term
children.label: 2 Term
children.text: An agreement entered into under paragraph (1) may require the borrower to remain employed as a prosecutor or public defender for less than three years.

children.label: A
children.text: giving priority to borrowers who have the least ability to repay their loans, except that the Attorney General shall determine a fair allocation of repayment benefits among prosecutors and public defenders, and among employing entities nationwide; and

children.label: B
children.text: subject to the availability of appropriations.

children.label: A
children.text: received repayment benefits under this section during the preceding fiscal year; and

children.label: B
children.text: has completed less than three years of the first required period of service specified for the borrower in an agreement entered into under subsection (d).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: g Regulations
children.text: The Attorney General is authorized to issue such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.
the cost of the program authorized under this section; and
the impact of such program on the hiring and retention of prosecutors and public defenders.

children.heading: GAO study
children.label: i GAO study
children.text: Not later than one year after , the Comptroller General shall conduct a study of, and report to Congress on, the impact that law school accreditation requirements and other factors have on the costs of law school and student access to law school, including the impact of such requirements on racial and ethnic minorities.August 14, 2008

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: j Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $25,000,000 for fiscal year 2009 and such sums as may be necessary for each of the five succeeding fiscal years.
